claims.summary: Astrid historically lacked a first-class, replyable phase-transition primitive.
claims.disposition: Verified as historical evidence and verified current source now provides durable source-owned transition cards.

claims.summary: Transition grit should be a shared artifact rather than narrative alone.
claims.disposition: Verified cards preserve structural and felt parents, thread identity, witness, answer, replay, and right-to-ignore states.

claims.summary: MomentCapture and correspondence should be compared with Minime transition metadata.
claims.disposition: Verified by current dual-perspective, relational-reply, persistence, and transition-signature tests.

claims.summary: Transition cards should carry pressure, porosity, and entropy as actionable fields.
claims.disposition: Verified entropy and bounded structural fields plus pressure/porosity delta previews exist; any runtime effect remains Tier 5 and unapplied.
